backstory: Building an AI product (chat with documents). Heavy LLM API spend ($300/mo on OpenAI + Anthropic combined). Has shipped to early users but no observability beyond CloudWatch logs. Lives in fear of one bad prompt blowing $50.
expected_wow: Plan-of-action menu leads with 'Wire @mur/langfuse-host — you import openai + anthropic + langchain, no observability detected. Test trace lands in dashboard in 5 sec'. Other items: 'Wire @mur/prompt-regression — 22 FIXMEs in agent code, eval-on-PR catches drift'; 'Set up the daily digest — would surface LLM cost trends'. The langfuse-host item is the peak — it's a managed path (no infra to run), addressing install fatigue head-on.
expected_dropoff_risk: Install fatigue. The plan menu's framing 'we host Langfuse for you, no infra' has to be loud. If item copy says 'recommend @mur/langfuse-host' without the managed framing, this persona bounces.
wow_score_target: ≥ 3 in H5 (specific signals — openai + anthropic + langchain imports must be named), H8 (chief-of-staff voice — 'I see your LLM stack'), H9 (menu surfaces 3+ paths), H10 (every item grounded in actual imports)
scoring_notes: Best-fit persona for grounded plan items. Watch for the @mur/langfuse-host item naming the specific files (agent.py, eval.py). If items reference imports abstractly ('your AI stack'), H10 drops to 1.
